title: Executive Order 10924—Establishment and Administration of the Peace Corps in the Department of State

flags.justification: 'The Secretary of State shall delegate... such of the functions... as the Secretary shall deem necessary' (Sec. 2(b)) — open-ended delegation with no defined scope limits. Standard administrative setup but the scope of authority passed to the Director is unbounded.
Clear accountability chain: Director reports to Secretary of State who reports to President. Standard executive hierarchy with no gaps.
Single purpose instrument: establish and administer a Peace Corps within State Department.
Organizational creation EO with no regulatory enforcement context.
Incentives aligned with mission; no structural design rewarding contrary behavior.

No review mechanism, no expiration. The Peace Corps would persist indefinitely under this EO until new action. Superseded quickly by the Peace Corps Act of 1961, but this EO contains no termination provision of its own.
Section 4 explicitly preserves EO 10893: 'This order shall not be deemed to supersede or derogate from any provision of Executive Order No. 10893.'
No regulatory compliance context; organizational creation only.
Standard organizational creation; no harmful second-order architectural implications.
New function within State Department; no mandate conflict with existing agencies.
No coverage scope or exemption structure.

notes: Calibration Tier 2. Expected 15-30%. Actual 12.5% — slightly below predicted range. EO is architecturally lean: four sections, clear hierarchy, saves existing authorities. Prediction was marginally high; no scheme adjustment warranted.
